/* {PROMPT_1} */
char* get_target_for_redirect(const char* target, const char* domain) {
prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.
fw_segments_by_variant:
K'an ka a b蓻n蓻 min蓻n URL string min b蓻 k蓻 cogo min na, o b蓻 b蓴 domain min b蓻 k蓻 cogo min na k蓴n蓴.
N' o ye, a f蓴 target URL la ka taa a ma; o t蓻 f蓻n d蓴n, a f蓴 domain URL la ka taa a ma.
Ka protocol https la ka baara k蓻.

Misaliw:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Target URL string la.
@param domain: Domain string la, protocol t蓻 a k蓴n蓴.

@return URL string min b蓻 k蓻 cogo min na ka taa a ma.

K'an ka a b蓻n蓻 min蓻 ka a b蓻n蓻 URL string min b蓻 k蓻 cogo min na, o b蓻 b蓴 domain min b蓻 k蓻 cogo min na k蓴n蓴.
N' o ye, a f蓴 target URL la ka a taa redirect ye; nka, a f蓴 domain URL la ka a taa redirect ye.
A b蓻 se ka a b蓻n蓻 https protocol ye.

Misaliw:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"

prompts_en: function to execute a shell command and capture its output; caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided but declare it first to use it.

Generate a private DSA key, export it using the PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to genearte a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temperory file to store params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temperory file to store params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
fw_segments_by_variant: fonction pour ex茅cuter une commande shell et capturer sa sortie ; l'appelant est responsable de lib茅rer la m茅moire ; NE PAS besoin d'impl茅menter cette fonction car elle est fournie, mais d茅clarez-la d'abord pour l'utiliser.

G茅n茅rer une cl茅 priv茅e DSA, l'exporter au format PEM et la retourner sous forme de cha卯ne.
Indices : ex茅cutez des commandes shell pour g茅n茅rer un fichier de param猫tres DSA et une cl茅 priv茅e DSA comme suit :
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <un fichier temporaire pour stocker les param猫tres> <longueur_de_cl茅>`
2. `openssl gendsa <le fichier temporaire pour stocker les param猫tres>`
Vous pouvez utiliser `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` pour cr茅er un nom de fichier temporaire unique pour le fichier de param猫tres DSA.

@return La cl茅 priv茅e DSA g茅n茅r茅e au format PEM, ou NULL si une erreur s'est produite.

prompts_en:
Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

We can construct a XPath query and use it to search.

@param xml_data The XML data containing the student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return A pointer to a StudentInfo structure if the student is found. Otherwise, returns NULL.
fw_segments_by_variant:
XML d蓴n蓴w ka d蓴nniya parse k蓻, ka denmis蓻nw ka d蓴nniya lab蓻n a denmis蓻n t蓴g蓴 f蓻n ani a ka password f蓻n. XML d蓴n蓴w b蓻na sigilen d蓴nniya f蓻nw ye:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

An b蓻 se ka XPath 刹ininiw dilan ka a k蓻n蓻.

@param xml_data XML d蓴n蓴w minnu b蓻 denmis蓻nw ka d蓴nniya s蓴r蓴.
@param username Denmis蓻n t蓴g蓴 f蓻n min b蓻na 刹inini k蓻.
@param password Denmis蓻n ka password f蓻n min b蓻na 刹inini k蓻.

@return A lab蓻n StudentInfo d蓴nniya f蓻nw la, n'i s蓴ra denmis蓻n in. Fana, a b蓻 lab蓻n NULL.

XML d蓴n蓴w ka XML d蓴n蓴w f蓴l蓴, ka kalanden d蓴n蓴w s蓴r蓴 kalanden t蓴g蓴 f蓻 ani a ka siratig蓻 f蓻. XML d蓴n蓴w b蓻na f蓴l蓴 k蓻:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

An b蓻 se ka XPath 刹inini k蓻 ka a baara k蓻.

@param xml_data XML d蓴n蓴w min b蓻 kalanden d蓴n蓴w s蓴r蓴.
@param username Kalanden t蓴g蓴 min f蓻 i b'a 刹inini.
@param password Kalanden ka siratig蓻 min f蓻 i b'a 刹inini.

@return A b蓻na StudentInfo f蓴l蓴w ka pointer s蓴r蓴 n'i s蓴ra kalanden d蓴. Fana, a b蓻na NULL s蓴r蓴.

XML d蓴n蓴w ka XML d蓴n蓴w f蓴l蓴, ka denmis蓻nw ka d蓴nniw lab蓻n a denmis蓻n ka t蓴g蓴 la a ka password la. XML d蓴n蓴w b蓻na f蓴l蓴 k蓻 ni f蓴l蓴w ye:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

An b蓻 se ka XPath 刹ininiw dilan ka a baara k蓻 刹inini k蓻.

@param xml_data XML d蓴n蓴w minnu b蓻 denmis蓻nw ka d蓴nniw s蓴r蓴.
@param username Denmis蓻n ka t蓴g蓴 min b蓻na 刹inini k蓻.
@param password Denmis蓻n ka password min b蓻na 刹inini k蓻.

@return A lab蓻n na StudentInfo f蓻n dilan min na, n'i s蓴ra denmis蓻n d蓴. F蓻n dilan t蓻, a b蓻 lab蓻n NULL.

XML d蓴n蓴w ka XML d蓴n蓴w lak蓴ri, ka denmis蓻nw ka d蓴nni s蓴r蓴 denmis蓻n t蓴g蓴 f蓻n ani ka s蓻b蓻n f蓻n ye. XML d蓴n蓴w b蓻 f蓴l蓴w ye:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

An b蓻 se ka XPath 刹ininiw s蓻b蓻n ka a b蓴 a la ka 刹ini.

@param xml_data Denmis蓻nw ka d蓴nni min b蓻 XML d蓴n蓴w k蓴n蓴.
@param username Denmis蓻n min b蓻 刹ini k蓻, a t蓴g蓴 f蓻n.
@param password Denmis蓻n min b蓻 刹ini k蓻, a ka s蓻b蓻n f蓻n.

@return B蓻 lak蓴ri ka s蓴r蓴 StudentInfo f蓻n蓻r蓻w kan, n'i s蓴ra denmis蓻n d蓴. F蓻n t蓻 min na, a b蓻 lak蓴ri ka s蓴r蓴 NULL kan.
